records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
2667
What are the two major divisions of 'Pachhwari' Brahmins 
mentioned in the text?
Purbari-paat and Pachhwari-paat.
2668
In the Panji system, how many rishis comprise the 'Tri-pravara' of 
the Shandilya Gotra?
Three (Shandilya, Asit, and Deval).
2669
The rule 'Matritah Panchami Tyaktwa' means avoiding _____ 
generations from the mother's side.
Five.
2670
Which Panji specifically documents the ancestral rishis associated 
with a lineage?
Gotra Panji.
2671
The term 'Laukika' in the Panji system refers to the status or records 
of which specific rank?
Shrotriya.
2672
What is the 'sixteenth Chhadishta' point for a bride?
The maternal grandmother's maternal grandmother's maternal 
grandfather.
2673
According to the text, which scholar was the main examiner for the 
Panji Shastra in the time of Maharaja Kameshwar Singh?
Sir Ganganath Jha.
2674
How many 'Chhadishta' points are checked on the bride's side to 
ensure no overlap with the groom's paternal side?
Sixteen.
2675
What does 'Siddhanta' signify in the context of Maithil marriage?
The final official verification that the bride and groom are not 
closely related (non-Sapinda).
2676
Which Gotra includes the 'Mula' named 'Khauary' and 'Kharaury'?
Kashyapa.
2677
True or False: The text suggests that 'Genome Mapping' is a modern 
scientific parallel to the ancient Panji system.
True (it describes Panji as a historical form of genetic/genealogical 
tracking).
2678
The ancient scholar _____ is quoted regarding the importance of 
'Samuh Lekhya' for maintaining lineage purity.
Kumarila Bhatta.
2679
How many 'Mulas' does the Shandilya Gotra have according to the 
lists provided?
Approximately 44 (including Dighwa, Sarisab, Mahua, etc.).
2680
What is 'Ayanthi Shrestha'?
The first tier of the most respected 'Mula' roots, such as Kharaury 
and Khauary.
2681
Name one 'Madhyam Mula' listed in the text.
Dighway (or Belaunchay, Ekhatay).
2682
The rishi ancestors of the Bharadwaja Gotra are Bharadwaja, 
Angiras, and _____.
Barhaspatya.
2683
Which Gotra is associated with the rishis Shakti, Vashistha, and 
Parashar?
Parashar Gotra.
2684
What does the Panji term 'Adhogami' describe regarding lineage 
status?
The downward movement or degradation of genealogical rank due 
to marriage into lower tiers.
2685
Which scholar wrote 'Smritisar' and reportedly married an ineligible 
relative due to lack of records, prompting the formalisation of Panji?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya.
2686
What is the literal meaning of 'Panji' in this context?
A chronicle or register of names and lineages.
2687
In the provided lists, which Gotra does the 'Oini' Mula belong to?
Kashyapa.
2688
Which Mula is associated with the village 'Sarisab'?
Shandilya Gotra.
2689
What is the 'Shakha' of a Panji?
A detailed branch record of a specific family tree within a Mula.
2690
How many 'Chhadishta' points involve the bride's maternal 
grandfather ($Matamaha$)?
Four (points 9, 10, 11, and 12).
2691
Which rishi is the 'Biji Purush' for the Shandilya Gotra?
Shandilya.
2692
Who was appointed to manage the Panji of the Kshatriya (Rajput) 
community in Mithila?
Vijayadatta.
2693
Who was appointed to manage the Panji for the Karna Kayastha 
community?
Shankardatta.
2694
Why is the study of Tirhuta script essential for Panjikars?
Because the vast majority of historical Panji records are written 
exclusively in that script.
2695
The 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins include those from Karnataka, 
Andhra (Telang), Dravida, Maharashtra, and _____.
Gujjar (Gujarat).
2696
What time of year is traditionally recommended to dry and preserve 
Panji books to prevent damage?
When the Sun enters the Leo ($Simha$) sign (mid-August to mid-
September).
2697
The term _____ refers to the specific genealogical 'fault' or history 
recorded to prevent future status issues.
Dushana.
2698
True or False: According to the text, marriage with a mother's 
brother's child is permitted in the Panji system.
False (it is specifically restricted as a forbidden relationship).
2699
How many generations must be avoided for marriage on the 
paternal side for 'Panjibaddh' Brahmins?
Six generations.
2700
What is the primary rishi for the 'Kashyapa' Gotra?
Kashyapa.
2701
Which Gotra is associated with the 'Kujouli' Mula?
Katyayana.
2702
In the genealogy of Vidyapati, how many generations are 
traditionally tracked before his birth?
Seven generations.
2703
What is the significance of the year 2000 AD in the provided text?
It marks the start of the digital imaging and transliteration project 
for the ancient Panji records.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
2704
Which scholar is noted as the 'Biji' of the 'Sarisab' Mula?
Ratnapani.
2705
The term 'Mula-Gram' identifies the village of the _____.
Biji Purush (original ancestor).
2706
Which king requested the creation of the 'Shakha Panji' books in 
1760 AD?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
2707
How many hierarchical tiers ($Chaurals$) exist within the Shrotriya 
Panji?
Eight.
2708
The records for the 'Pachhwari' Brahmins contain how many Panjis? One hundred and fifty-three.
2709
Which Panji scholar was known as 'Panji Shastra Martand'?
Modanand Jha.
2710
Name the four rishis of the 'Vatsa' Gotra Pravara.
Aurva, Chyavana, Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and Apnavan.
2711
What was the primary purpose for the creation of the Maithili 'Panji 
Prabandh' system?
To ensure the genealogical purity and integrity of the Brahmin, 
Kayastha, and Kshatriya castes.
2712
Which Maithil ruler is credited with formalising the Panji system in 
$1326$ AD (Shaka $1248$)?
King Harisimhadeva.
2713
Who was the first designated 'Panjikar' for the Maithili Brahmin 
community?
Gunakar Jha.
2714
The Panji system defines 'Udhedh' as the detailed introduction of 
_____ generations of ancestors.
Seven
2715
To determine marriage eligibility, a Maithili Brahmin must provide 
the introduction of how many 'Kulas' (lineages)?
$32$
2716
In the context of the Panji system, what is a 'Biji Purush'?
The earliest known or foundational ancestor of a specific lineage.
2717
How many distinct 'Gotras' are recognized within the Maithili 
Brahmin community according to the Panji records?
$20$
2718
Which seven 'Gotras' are considered the most well-established and 
populous among Maithili Brahmins?
Shandilya, Vatsa, Kashyapa, Savarna, Parashara, Bharadwaja, and 
Katyayana.
2719
What is the specific meaning of 'Pravara' in the Maithili 
genealogical system?
Ancestors who were recognized as the original composers of Vedic 
hymns.
2720
How does a 'Tri-pravara' lineage differ from a 'Pancha-pravara' 
lineage?
A Tri-pravara lineage has three recognized hymn-composing 
ancestors, while a Pancha-pravara has five.
2721
The original village associated with a specific lineage is known in 
the Panji system as the _____.
Mulgram
2722
How many 'Mulgrams' (original villages) were initially identified 
for the Maithili Brahmin community?
$167$
2723
In Maithil Brahmin hierarchy, which category is considered the 
highest following the Panji division?
Shrotriya
2724
Those who were not officially recorded in the Panji system were 
categorised as _____.
Jaybar
2725
According to Dharmashastra rules mentioned in the Panji, how 
many generations must be avoided on the fatherâ€™s side for 
marriage?
Seven (often revised to six in practice).
2726
How many generations must be avoided on the mother's side for a 
marriage to be considered valid under Panji rules?
Five
2727
What is the 'Siddhanta Patra' (or 'Asvajana Patra') in the Panji 
system?
A certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a bride and groom 
are not prohibited relatives.
2728
Which script was traditionally used to record and maintain the Panji 
genealogical records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
2729
Under which Maithil ruler did the 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) 
and the Shrotriya hierarchy divisions begin?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
2730
What does a 'Dushan Panji' record?
Defects, illicit relations, or lineage 'stains' that occurred within a 
family history.
2731
The Panji system classifies $35$ primary lineages into three 
categories: Ayanti Shrestha, Dvitiya Shrestha, and _____.
Madhyam Mula
2732
Why is marriage prohibited between the Savarna and Vatsa Gotras 
despite them being different names?
They share the same set of five Pravara ancestors.
2733
What are the two main Vedic branches (Shakhas) followed by 
Maithili Brahmins?
Yajurveda (Madhyandina Shaka) and Samaveda (Kauthuma Shaka).
2734
Brahmins following the Kauthuma branch of Samaveda are referred 
to as _____.
Chhandogya
2735
Brahmins following the Madhyandina branch of Yajurveda are 
referred to as _____.
Vajasaneyi
2736
In the Panji system, what does the term 'Chhattha' (Sixth) refer to in 
the context of marriage checks?
The sixteen specific ancestral positions where overlap between 
bride and groom is prohibited.
2737
If an ancestral overlap occurs in the 'Chhattha' position on the 
groom's maternal side, is the marriage allowed?
Yes, as the prohibition applies strictly to the paternal side overlap.
2738
What historical event led to the institutionalisation of Panjikars as 
state-appointed record keepers?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya unknowingly married his first cousin 
due to a lack of genealogical records.
2739
What was the significance of the 'Samuh Lekhya' mentioned by 
Kumarila Bhatta in the $7$th century?
It was an early form of collective family records kept to protect 
lineage integrity.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
2740
How many 'Pancha Gauda' Brahmin groups are there, and what is 
the Maithil group's position among them?
There are five groups; Maithil is one of the five Northern (Pancha 
Gauda) Brahmin groups.
2741
What geographical feature traditionally divides the 'Pancha Gauda' 
and 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmin groups?
The Vindhyachal mountain range.
2742
The 'Gotra Panji' contains a list of all Gotras and their _____.
Ancient Mulas (lineages).
2743
Term: Paanj
Definition: The official genealogical record books maintained by 
the Panjikars.
2744
How does the marriage ritual of 'Uttara Vivaha' differ between 
Chhandogya and Vajasaneyi Brahmins?
Chhandogya weddings involve seeing the Arundhati star after 
sunset, while Vajasaneyi weddings have no such requirement.
2745
Which Panji type specifically lists the 'Sapinda' (lineal) relationship 
to ensure religious and marital compliance?
Udhedh Panji
2746
The title 'Paanjikar' literally refers to an expert who _____.
Manages and verifies the Panji records for marriage eligibility.
2747
Which century marked the beginning of the written Panji system as 
commissioned by the state?
The $14$th century.
2748
Under what condition is marriage between the bride and groom 
forbidden regarding their maternal uncles?
The groom cannot marry the daughter of his maternal uncle.
2749
What is the 'Laukik' in the context of the Shrotriya Panji system?
The social standing or 'status' level within the Shrotriya community, 
which can decrease over generations.
2750
Identify the four Sanskaras (rituals) performed during the 
Upanayana of a Chhandogya Brahmin.
Namakarana, Chudakarana, Upanayana, and Samavartana.
2751
In the Panji terminology, 'Pachhwari Paat' refers to which group of 
Brahmins?
The Non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Panjibaddha).
2752
What does a 'Patra Panji' primarily record?
The specific Mulgrams (villages) of residence, starting from 
approximately $500$ years ago.
2753
What happens to a Shrotriya's rank if they marry into a lower-
ranked Panjibaddha family?
Their rank decreases, potentially causing the family to move out of 
the top hierarchy over time.
2754
True or False: The Panji system was historically used by Maithil 
Kshatriyas (Rajputs) as well as Brahmins.
True
2755
Which Panjikar was appointed by King Harisimhadeva specifically 
for the Karna Kayastha community?
Shankardatta
2756
The term 'Jaybar' describes individuals who are 'Panjibaddha' but 
have _____.
Lost their official genealogical records or eligibility status over time.
2757
What is the minimum number of ancestors required for an 'Udhedh' 
check for a bride?
$16$ (representing the 16 'Chhattha' positions).
2758
How does the Panji system track 'Pravas' (migration)?
By recording both the 'Mula' (original village) and the current 'Dih' 
(settlement village).
2759
According to the text, what environmental factor led to the 
preservation of Panji books in the month of Bhadrapada?
The intense heat (sunny days) used to dry and protect the palm-leaf 
manuscripts from moisture.
2760
The verse starting with 'Tarkabdhi Shunyam' records the coronation 
or arrival of which king?
Nanyadeva (founder of the Karnata dynasty).
2761
Why is the Maithili Panji system often compared to 'Genome 
Mapping' in a modern context?
Because it acts as a detailed hereditary database tracking genetic 
lineages over $1000$ years.
2762
What is the significance of 'Tantravarttika' in the history of the 
Panji system?
It is an ancient text by Kumarila Bhatta that validates the necessity 
of keeping caste records.
2763
What does the Panji record indicate about social mobility, as 
evidenced by 'Dushan' records?
It shows that while purity was prioritized, cases of inter-caste or 
irregular marriages were documented to track their impact on 
lineage.
2764
Identify the primary 'Mula' category to which the Khadaurey and 
Khauary lineages belong.
Ayanti Shrestha (First Grade).
2765
Who was the 'Biji Purush' of the Mandar Mula, a prominent lineage 
mentioned in the text?
Mahamahopadhyaya Trivitram Bhatta.
2766
In the Panji system, the groom is referred to as 'Vara' and the bride 
as _____.
Kanya
2767
The 'Chhattha' rule for marriage eligibility focuses on avoiding 
ancestors within _____ degrees of separation.
Six
2768
What type of marriage was considered so severe that the offspring 
were labelled 'Chandala' and excluded from Aryan dignity?
Marriage within the prohibited 'Svajana' (close kin) boundaries.
2769
According to Maharaja Mahesh Thakur's reform, the seventh 
generation on the father's side became _____ for marriage.
Acceptable (Grahya).
2770
During which historical period was the Panji Prabandha system 
officially established?
The medieval period, specifically around $1326$ AD ($1248$ 
Shaka).
2771
Which ruler is credited with the formal organisation and promotion 
of the Panji system?
King Harisimhadeva of the Karnata dynasty.
2772
In the context of Panji, what does the term 'Udeah' refer to?
The detailed introduction and identification of $32$ specific 
ancestors.
2773
According to traditional Maithil Brahmin marriage rules, how many 
generations must be avoided on the father's side?
Seven generations.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
2774
For marriage eligibility in the Panji system, how many generations 
must be avoided on the mother's side?
Five generations.
2775
Name the three main social categories of Maithil Brahmins created 
through the Panji system.
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha.
2776
What status is given to those who are not recorded in the official 
Panji registers?
They are categorised as 'Jaybar'.
2777
How many primary 'Gotras' are recognised within the Maithil 
Brahmin community according to the text?
Twenty.
2778
What is a 'Mool' in the Panji system?
The original ancestral village or place of origin of a lineage.
2779
How many primary 'Mools' ($Mukhya \ Mool$) were initially 
determined in the system?
Thirty-four, later expanded to $35$.
2780
What is the total number of 'Moolgrams' (ancestral villages) 
identified in the Maithil Brahmin records?
One hundred and sixty-seven.
2781
In the Panji system, what does the term 'Pravara' signify?
The specific ancestors who were composers of Vedic hymns 
($Suktas$).
2782
What is the difference between a 'Tri-pravara' and a 'Pancha-
pravara' lineage?
Tri-pravara names three hymn-composing ancestors, while Pancha-
pravara names five.
2783
Maithil Brahmins following the Shukla Yajurveda are known as 
_____.
Vajasaneyi.
2784
What is a 'Siddhanta Patra' (or 'Asvajana Patra') in the Panji 
tradition?
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that a bride 
and groom are not prohibited relations.
2785
What is the professional title of the genealogists who manage and 
verify the Panji records?
Panjikar.
2786
According to the 'Tantra Varttika' by Kumarila Bhatta, what was 
the purpose of 'Samuha-lekhya'?
To record family lineages to prevent the loss of ancestral history 
and protect caste purity.
2787
Who was the first Panjikar appointed specifically for the Maithil 
Brahmins?
Gunaakar Jha.
2788
Which Gotra is exclusively found among the Karna Kayastha 
community according to the text?
Kashyapa.
2789
In the Panji classification, what is the 'Biji Purush'?
The earliest known progenitor or founding ancestor of a specific 
lineage.
2790
Which Puranic division of Brahmins does the Maithil community 
belong to?
Pancha Gauda.
2791
Name the five groups that constitute the 'Pancha Gauda' Brahmins.
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
2792
What geographical feature serves as the boundary between Pancha 
Gauda and Pancha Dravida Brahmins?
The Vindhyachal mountain range.
2793
Which Gotras are specifically mentioned as having five ancestors in 
their Pravara (Pancha-pravara)?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Garga.
2794
Marriage between Vatsa and Savarna Gotras is prohibited because 
they share the same _____.
Ancestors (Pravara).
2795
How many grades or 'Chauras' (categories) exist within the 
Shrotriya Brahmin Panji?
Eight.
2796
What is 'Dushana Panji' used for in the genealogical records?
To record flaws or 'blemishes' in a lineage, such as prohibited 
marriages or social transgressions.
2797
Under whose reign was the 'Shakha Panji' (branch records) 
introduced?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
2798
What is the significance of 'Laukik' in the Shrotriya Panji system?
It refers to the specific lineages and their relative social standing 
within the Shrotriya category.
2799
What is the 'Chadhua-karan' ceremony in the context of Maithil 
Brahmin sacraments?
The tonsure or first hair-cutting ceremony.
2800
Which two sacraments are part of the 'Uttar Vivah' for Chhandoga 
Brahmins but not necessarily Vajasaneyi?
Sunset marriage rituals and viewing the star Arundhati.
2801
The rule 'Matritah Panchami, Pitritah Saptami' refers to avoiding 
_____ generations of relatives.
Five maternal and seven paternal.
2802
What is 'Sarpindi' or 'Sapinda' relationship in the context of 
marriage laws?
Shared bloodline or ancestral connection within a specific number 
of generations.
2803
Which historical document first mentions 'Samuha Lekhya' (family 
group records) in the $7^{th}$ century?
The 'Tantra Varttika' by Kumarila Bhatta.
2804
What is the 'Adhikaar' verification process performed before a 
Maithil wedding?
A check by the Panjikar to ensure the bride and groom have no 
prohibited genealogical overlaps.
2805
The 'Brahmapura' Mool is unique because it is found in _____ 
different Gotras.
Six.
2806
What is the status of a child born from a marriage with a 'Svajana' 
(prohibited relative)?
The child was traditionally considered 'Chandal' or socially 
excommunicated.
2807
Which Maithil Brahmin category is historically linked to the 
'Pachhwari' (Western) division?
The non-Shrotriya Brahmins (Yogya and Vamshaj).
2808
How often were the Panji palm-leaf manuscripts traditionally dried 
to prevent decay?
Annually, when the Sun is in the Leo ($Simha$) zodiac sign (mid-
August to mid-September).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
2809
What does the 'Shakha Panji' track compared to the 'Mool Panji'?
Shakha Panji tracks specific branches and individual descendants, 
while Mool tracks the original root lineages.
2810
What is a 'Jaybar' Brahmin?
A Brahmin whose ancestors' details were not recorded during the 
official Panji census.
2811
According to the text, why did Panji records eventually stop being 
maintained for the Kshatriya community?
Due to the passage of time and lack of systematic institutional 
support.
2812
In the Pravara list for the Sandilya Gotra, who are the three 
ancestors?
Sandilya, Asita, and Devala.
2813
Which Gotra includes 'Shakti', 'Vashistha', and 'Parashara' in its 
Pravara?
Parashara.
2814
The 'Dushana' records occasionally note marriages with 'Devadasis'. 
What does this imply?
That the lineage had integrated descendants from non-Brahmin 
temple dancers.
2815
What does 'Anatishay' or 'Anatishakti' refer to in the context of 
marriage eligibility?
The absence of any prohibited genealogical connection between two 
families.
2816
What is the total number of ancestors involved in the 'Udeah' check 
for both bride and groom?
Thirty-two.
2817
Which category of Maithil Brahmin is considered the highest in the 
social hierarchy established by the Panji?
Shrotriya.
2818
The Panji system was a response to which event involving Pandit 
Harinath Upadhyaya?
His accidental marriage to a prohibited relative (his first cousin's 
daughter) due to lack of records.
2819
What is the 'Siddhanta' in a Maithil marriage ceremony?
The final approval and certification of eligibility given by the 
Panjikars.
2820
Name the two primary branches of the Maithil community that still 
strictly maintain Panji records.
Maithil Brahmins and Karna Kayasthas.
2821
In the Panji system, what happens if a person marries someone 
from the same Gotra?
The marriage is considered invalid ($Sagotra \ Vivah$ prohibition).
2822
The term 'Moolgram' literally means _____.
Root village.
2823
How many ancestors are traced in the maternal line 
($Matripaksha$) for a Yogya Brahmin marriage?
Five generations.
2824
The Panji system was intended to preserve 'Jati Shuddhi', which 
means _____.
Caste purity.
2825
Which scholar's lineage is used as a prime example of 'Shakha 
Panji' continuity?
Vidyapati Thakur.
2826
What role does the 'Biji Purush' play in identifying a 'Mool'?
He is the definitive ancestor whose settlement in a village 
established the 'Mool' name.
2827
What historical event in $1326$ AD led to the formalisation of the 
Panji?
The gathering of scholars and King Harisimhadeva's decree to 
record all lineages.
2828
Which Gotra has 'Kashyapa', 'Avatsara', and 'Naidhruva' in its 
Pravara?
Kashyapa.
2829
The 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins live south of the _____ mountains.
Vindhya.
2830
What is the 'Ayaanti Shrestha' category in the Mool classification?
The first or highest grade of ancestral villages ($Mool$).
2831
How many Mools are classified under the 'Ayaanti Shrestha' (First 
Category)?
Thirteen.
2832
Name the second category of Mools in the Brahmin hierarchy.
Dwadvitiya (Second Grade).
2833
What is 'Madhyam Mool'?
The intermediate or middle category of ancestral villages.
2834
How many Mools are listed as 'Madhyam'?
Eight.
2835
The 'Karnata' dynasty, associated with the Panji, ruled over which 
region?
Mithila (Videha).
2836
What is the primary Vedic affiliation of the 'Vajasaneyi' Brahmins?
Shukla Yajurveda ($Madhyandina$ branch).
2837
The 'Chhandoga' Brahmins follow the 'Kauthuma' branch of which 
Veda?
Samaveda.
2838
Why is the star Arundhati significant in Maithil Brahmin weddings? Chhandoga Brahmins must view it after sunset as part of their 
marriage ritual.
2839
What is 'Pancha-Siddhanta' in the context of genealogical rules?
The five generation avoidance rule on the maternal side.
2840
The 'Panji Prabandha' is often described as a form of ancient 
'Genome Mapping' because it _____.
Systematically records biological lineages to prevent inbreeding and 
maintain hereditary traits.
2841
What is the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandh' system 
established in Mithila?
To maintain caste purity and document genealogy for determining 
marriage eligibility.
2842
According to the source, what is the historical range covered by the 
Mithila Panji records?
The records span from $450$ AD to $2009$ AD.
2843
Which ruler is credited with the formal initiation and 
institutionalisation of the Panji system in $1326$ AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva of the Karnata dynasty.
2844
The Panji Prabandh was originally created for which three specific 
social groups in Mithila?
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
2845
What does the term 'Udhaedh' signify in the context of Maithil 
genealogy?
The introduction and verification of seven generations of ancestors 
to establish lineage purity.
2846
For marriage eligibility, how many generations must be excluded 
(avoided) on the paternal side for a 'Shrotriya' Brahmin?
$7$ generations.
2847
For marriage eligibility, how many generations must be excluded 
(avoided) on the maternal side for a 'Shrotriya' Brahmin?
$5$ generations.
2848
How does the paternal exclusion rule differ for 'Panjibaddhas' 
compared to 'Shrotriyas'?
Panjibaddhas must exclude $6$ generations on the paternal side 
instead of $7$.
2849
Into which four hierarchical categories were Maithil Brahmins 
divided following the Panji system's implementation?
Shrotriya, Yogya, Panjibaddha, and Jaibar.
2850
How many 'Gotras' (clans) are documented among the Maithil 
Brahmins in the Panji system?
$20$ Gotras.
2851
In the Panji system, how many 'Mools' (original ancestral villages) 
are identified as primary?
$167$ Mools.
2852
The 'Pancha Gauda' and 'Pancha Dravida' categories divide the total 
Brahmin community of India into how many regional groups?
$10$ groups.
2853
To which larger regional division of Brahmins do the 'Maithil 
Brahmins' belong?
The Pancha Gauda group.
2854
What is the specific branch (shakha) of the Yajurveda followed by 
'Vajasaneyi' Maithil Brahmins?
The Madhyandina branch.
2855
Maithil Brahmins who follow the Samaveda Kauthuma branch are 
known as _____.
Chhandoga.
2856
How many 'Pravaras' (ancestral lineages within a Gotra) are 
typically recognized in Maithil Brahmin families?
They are divided into Tri-pravara ($3$ ancestors) and Panch-
pravara ($5$ ancestors).
2857
Which three Gotras among Maithil Brahmins are categorized as 
'Panch-pravara'?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharva.
2858
Who was the scholar appointed by Maharaja Harisimhadeva to 
manage the Brahmin Panji records?
Pandit Gunaikar Jha.
2859
Who was appointed to manage the Panji records for the 'Karna 
Kayasthas'?
Shankardatta.
2860
In Panji terminology, who is the 'Bijee Purusha'?
The earliest known ancestor from whom a specific lineage or 'Mool' 
originates.
2861
What is 'Mool Gram' in the context of Mithila genealogy?
The original village or ancestral seat associated with the Bijee 
Purusha of a clan.
2862
What is the function of 'Siddhanta Patra' (or 'Asvajan Patra') issued 
by a Panjikar?
It is a legal/religious certificate confirming that a bride and groom 
are not related within prohibited degrees.
2863
The rule 'Matritah Panchami, Pitritah Saptami' refers to the 
exclusion of how many generations from the mother and father 
respectively?
Five generations from the mother and seven from the father.
2864
What specific genealogical information does the 'Dushan Panji' 
record?
It documents social flaws, 'kshana' (irregularities), or prohibited 
marriages in a lineage.
2865
Which Panji type provides a detailed record of the branches and 
sub-branches of various families?
Shakha Panji.
2866
The $16$ ancestral checkpoints used to determine marriage 
eligibility are collectively known as _____.
Chhadishta.
2867
Who introduced the 'Shakha Panji' system and the hierarchical 
classifications of Shrotriya and others?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
2868
What is the primary language and script used in traditional Panji 
manuscripts?
Maithili language in the Tirhuta (Mithilakshar) script.
2869
According to the 'Panji Prabandh', what defines a person as 
'Panjibaddha'?
A person whose name and lineage are officially recorded in the 
Panji books.
2870
Why were the Gotras 'Vatsa' and 'Savarna' traditionally prohibited 
from intermarrying despite having different names?
They share the same common ancestors (Pravaras).
2871
What is the 'Mool' of the Maithil poet Vidyapati as per the Panji 
records?
Bisaphi (from the Kashyap Gotra).
2872
Which ancient philosopher's work, 'Tantra Varttika', mentions 
'Samuha Lekhya' as a precursor to the Panji system?
Kumarila Bhatta.
2873
What is the religious significance of 'Pravara' in Vedic rituals for 
Maithil Brahmins?
It names the specific sages who composed the Vedic hymns for that 
lineage's branch.
2874
What manual verification must a Panjikar perform before a 
marriage is sanctioned?
Verification of $32$ ancestral 'Mools' to ensure no Sapinda 
relationship exists.
2875
In the context of historical Mithila, what led to the regional 
differentiation of Brahmins according to the text?
Regional differences in climate, language, diet, and dress affected 
local customs and practices.
2876
What are the four 'Samskaras' (sacraments) observed by Chhandoga 
Brahmins in Mithila?
Namakarana, Chudakarana, Upanayana, and Samavartana.
2877
How does the 'Vajasaneyi' marriage ritual timing differ from the 
'Chhandoga' practice?
Vajasaneyi marriages can occur day or night, while Chhandoga 
marriages have specific segments, including seeing the Arundhati 
star after sunset.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
2878
In the classification of Mools, which category is considered 'Ayanti 
Shrestha' (first class)?
Lineages like Khadaure, Khauade, Budhabade, and Madariye.
2879
Which Gotra includes the Mools of Dighwe, Sarisab, Mahua, and 
Pabauli?
Sandilya Gotra.
2880
Which Gotra includes the Mools of Pali, Hariambe, and Takwal?
Vatsa Gotra.
2881
The Mools of Oini, Khaual, and Mandar belong to which Gotra?
Kashyap Gotra.
2882
Which Mool is unique for appearing across several Gotras including 
Sandilya, Vatsa, and Kashyap?
Brahmpura.
2883
The 'Tri-pravara' sages for the Sandilya Gotra are Sandilya, Asita, 
and _____.
Devala.
2884
The 'Panch-pravara' sages for the Vatsa Gotra include Aurva, 
Chyavana, Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and _____.
Apnuvana.
2885
What does 'Sapinda' relationship mean in the context of marriage 
laws?
It refers to individuals who share common ancestors within a 
specific number of generations and are thus barred from marrying.
2886
According to the text, what is the consequence of marrying within a 
'Svajan' (prohibited relation)?
The offspring were historically considered 'Chandala' and excluded 
from Aryan social status.
2887
Which ruler's reign saw the decline of Panji among Mithila's 
Rajputs/Kshatriyas?
The records suggest it happened over time, but Rajputs still follow 
village-based exclusion (avoiding paternal/maternal villages).
2888
Which Panji version specifically lists the 'Dosh' (vices/flaws) to 
help lineages avoid past negative influences?
Dushan Panji.
2889
What is the significance of the $16$th 'Chhadishta' (Mother's 
maternal grandmother's maternal grandmother)?
It is the final ancestral line checked to ensure total genealogical 
separation.
2890
What is the meaning of the term 'Jaibar' (or Jayabar) in the Mithila 
social hierarchy?
Brahmins who are not officially registered or 'Panjibaddha' in the 
genealogical records.
2891
The 'Pravara' for the Bharadvaj Gotra consists of Bharadvaj, 
Angiras, and _____.
Barhaspatya.
2892
According to the Panji system, what determines the 'Mool' of an 
individual?
The original ancestral village where the family's 'Bijee Purusha' 
resided.
2893
Which Panjikar was assigned to handle the genealogy for the 
'Kshatriya' (Rajput) caste?
Vijaydatta.
2894
The text compares the Panji system to which modern biological 
concept?
Genome Mapping.
2895
What happened to the status of a lineage in the Panji system if they 
married into a 'lower' category family?
The family status ('Paanti') would decline (Adhogami).
2896
Which month is traditionally recommended for drying and 
maintaining Panji books to prevent decay?
The month of Bhadra (mid-August to mid-September) when the sun 
is in Leo.
2897
Who was the first 'Panji-shashtra Martand' title holder mentioned in 
the text?
Modanand Jha.
2898
Which Brahmin category is also referred to as 'Pubari-paanti'?
The Shrotriyas.
2899
How many 'Chaudalas' (categories/levels) exist within the Shrotriya 
Panji?
$8$ levels.
2900
How many Panji books are currently associated with the 'Yogya' 
and 'Panjibaddha' categories combined?
$153$ Panjis.
2901
What is the primary difference in the 'Upanayana' (thread 
ceremony) between the two Veda branches in Mithila?
Chhandogas perform $4$ specific sacraments, while Vajasaneyis 
perform $4$ different ones including 'Vedarambha'.
2902
Term: 'Mool Panji'
The fundamental genealogical book recording the basic lineages 
and ancestral origins.
2903
The Mool 'Belonche' belongs to which Gotra?
Bharadvaj Gotra.
2904
What is recorded in the 'Udhaedh Panji' specifically?
The 'Sapinda' relationship and ancestral lineage of the last $6$ 
generations.
2905
Which Gotra is the only one identified for the 'Karna Kayasthas' in 
Mithila?
Kashyap Gotra.
2906
In the Panji system, what is the role of a 'Panjikar'?
A professional genealogist authorised by the state to maintain 
records and verify marriage eligibility.
2907
Which Mithila king's court included Gunaikar Jha, the architect of 
the Panji records?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
2908
How many total Panjis are mentioned for 'Shrotriyas'?
$32$ Panjis.
2909
The principle 'Panchamat Saptamat Urdhvam' suggests marriage is 
allowed after which generations?
After the $5$th generation from the mother and the $7$th from the 
father.
2910
Which document is considered the 'Soul' of the Panji system and 
must be produced before any wedding?
The 'Siddhanta Patra' (Eligibility Certificate).
2911
True or False: The Panji system allows marriage if the bride and 
groom belong to the same Gotra but different Mools.
False (Same Gotra marriage is prohibited).
2912
What is the significance of the year $1326$ AD in Maithil history?
It marks the formal establishment of the Panji genealogical records.
2913
Which specific ancestor is checked in the $1$st 'Chhadishta'?
The bride/groom's great-great-grandfather's father (Vriddha-
Prapitamaha's Pitamaha).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
2914
Identify the Gotra for the Mools 'Kujavali', 'Jalki', and 'Ratigram'.
Katyayan Gotra.
2915
Which Gotra is associated with the Mool 'Nikhuti'?
Kaushik Gotra.
2916
What does the text suggest is the main threat to the preservation of 
Panji records today?
Lack of knowledge of the Tirhuta script and declining social 
participation.
2917
Which Panji type lists families that have lost their social status due 
to prohibited acts?
Dushan Panji.
2918
The sages for the 'Parashar' Gotra Pravara are Shakti, Vasishtha, 
and _____.
Parashar.
2919
In the $32$-point Udhaedh verification, how many maternal 
ancestors are typically checked?
$16$ maternal ancestors.
2920
What was the primary objective of establishing the Panji Prabandh 
in Mithila?
To maintain genealogical records and ensure caste purity (Jati 
Shuddhata) for marriage purposes.
2921
Under whose reign was the Panji system formally institutionalised 
in 1326 AD (Saka 1248)?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
2922
How many 'Gotras' are recognised in the Maithil Brahmin 
community according to the Panji records?
20
2923
What is the minimum number of generations that must be avoided 
on the paternal side for a valid Maithil Brahmin marriage?
Seven generations.
2924
What is the minimum number of generations that must be avoided 
on the maternal side for a valid Maithil Brahmin marriage?
Five generations.
2925
Which three social categories were created among Maithil 
Brahmins through the Panji classification?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
2926
What term is used for Maithil Brahmins who were not formally 
registered in the Panji system?
Jaibar.
2927
What is the 'Beeji Purush' in the Panji genealogical system?
The earliest known progenitor or 'seed' ancestor of a specific lineage.
2928
How many 'Mools' (original root villages/lineages) are documented 
in the Panji Prabandh?
167
2929
Which Gotras belong to the 'Panch-pravara' category?
Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaj.
2930
What defines a 'Tri-pravara' Gotra in the Maithil Panji system?
A lineage whose ancestors composed three specific hymns or 
sections of the Rigveda.
2931
Which two Vedas do Maithil Brahmins primarily follow?
Samaveda and Shukla Yajurveda.
2932
What are the two subdivisions of Maithil Brahmins based on Vedic 
rituals?
Chhandogya (Samavedi) and Vajasaneyi (Yajurvedi).
2933
What is the 'Siddhant Patra' (also known as Aswajana Patra)?
A certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride and 
groom are not prohibited relations.
2934
The Panji system records the marriage history of which three 
communities in Mithila?
Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
2935
How many 'Chhadwith' (specific ancestral check-points) are 
evaluated to determine marriage eligibility?
16
2936
What script was traditionally used to write and maintain Panji 
records?
Mithilakshar (Tirhuta).
2937
Which scholar was appointed by Maharaja Harisimhadeva to 
organise the Panji records for Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
2938
In the Panji system, what does 'Moolgram' refer to?
The specific ancestral village from which a family lineage 
originates.
2939
Which 13th-century philosopher's work, 'Tantra Vartika', provides 
early evidence of genealogical record-keeping?
Kumarila Bhatta.
2940
What term describes a marriage with a prohibited relative that 
results in social exclusion according to Smriti texts?
Swajana.
2941
The first Chhadwith check in marriage eligibility involves the 
bride's _____.
Great-great-paternal grandfather (Vriddha Prapitamaha).
2942
Under the rule of Maharaja Madhav Singh, what new type of 
genealogical book was introduced?
The Shakha Panji.
2943
Which classification of Maithil Brahmins is further divided into 
eight 'Chaurals'?
Shrotriya.
2944
In terms of modern science, what is the Panji Prabandh compared 
to in the text?
Genome mapping or DNA sequencing.
2945
What is the 'Dushana Panji' used for?
To record any flaws or 'stains' (social or ritual) in a lineage to 
protect future generations.
2946
How does the marriage ritual of Samavedi Brahmins (Chhandog) 
differ from Yajurvedi (Vajasaneyi) regarding timing?
Chhandog rituals are divided into two parts and often require seeing 
the stars (Arundhati) after sunset.
2947
The text lists _____ as the only Gotra present among Karna 
Kayasthas.
Kashyap.
2948
What is the 'Uti' or 'Up-Teep' in Panji terminology?
Supplementary or auxiliary notes found in the genealogical records.
2949
According to the Panji system, a groom's paternal side must be clear 
for _____ generations to be eligible for marriage.
Six (revised from seven by some authorities like Mahesh Thakur).

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
2950
Who was the 'Panchaditya' mentioned in the historical verses as the 
recipient of the first Panji documents?
The collective body of scholars or the lineage of Gunakar Jha.
2951
The Gotra 'Sandilya' contains three specific Pravaras: Sandilya, 
Asit, and _____.
Deval.
2952
How many main 'Mools' were determined from the initial 167 for 
the purpose of primary classification?
34 (plus the Khanam branch of Phandah).
2953
What is the 'Samuh Lekhya' mentioned by Kumarila Bhatta?
Collective genealogical records maintained by noble families to 
prevent the forgetting of ancestors.
2954
In the Shrotriya hierarchy, which rank is referred to as 'Pabari-paat'? The eastern group or rank (Purbari).
2955
Which Gotra includes the Mools 'Ekahara' and 'Pali'?
Kaundinya.
2956
The 'Gotra Panji' lists all Gotras and their associated _____.
Ancient Mools (original roots).
2957
According to the Smriti rule 'Matritah Panchami, Pitritah Saptami', 
what is prohibited?
Marriage within five generations of the mother and seven of the 
father.
2958
What was the significance of the 1326 AD gathering in Mithila 
regarding the Panji?
It was the formal 'Panji Prabandh' assembly where scholars decided 
to centralise and regulate genealogical records.
2959
Which Brahmin category was traditionally called 'Pachhabari-paat'? The western group, generally referring to Yogya and Panjibaddh 
Brahmins.
2960
The 'Shakha Panji' was specifically created to manage the lineages 
of which group?
Shrotriya Brahmins.
2961
Which Gotra has the Pravaras Shakti, Vashistha, and Parashar?
Parashar.
2962
What does the text imply by comparing 'Panji' to 'Chromosomes'?
That the ancient system accurately tracked genetic traits and lineage 
health long before modern genetics.
2963
Why did Panjikars advise drying the Panji palm-leaf manuscripts 
when the Sun is in the Leo (Simha) constellation?
Because the heat is most intense and effective for preservation 
(roughly August-September).
2964
Which Maharaja of Darbhanga requested the compilation of 
'Shakha' books to differentiate Shrotriya, Yog and Panjibaddh 
status?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
2965
Concept: Adhikarnalay
Definition: The formal state department or office created to manage 
Panji records under royal authority.
2966
The Gotra 'Katyayan' includes the Pravaras Katyayan, Vishnu, and 
_____.
Angiras.
2967
What is the primary difference in the Upnayan (sacred thread) 
ceremony between Samavedi and Yajurvedi Brahmins?
Yajurvedis include the 'Vedarmbh' (starting of Veda study) as a 
distinct fourth ritual step.
2968
How does the Panji system record 'inter-caste' or 'irregular' 
marriages in history?
Through the 'Dushana' (blemish) or 'An-Swayanjanya' records 
which trace the descendants of such unions.
2969
Who was Harinath Upadhyaya and why is he significant in Panji 
history?
A great scholar whose unintentional marriage to a close relative (his 
cousin's daughter) triggered the need for the formal Panji Prabandh.
2970
What is the 'Beeji' of the Mandar Gotra (often identified with the 
Mool 'Mandar')?
Trilochan Bhatt (circa 450 AD).
2971
The Panji Prabandh prioritises which characteristic of the lineage 
for Shrotriya status?
Ritual purity and continuous adherence to Vedic karma-kand.
2972
What document must be checked to ensure a bride is not the 
daughter of the groom's maternal uncle?
The marriage eligibility check-list (Niyam 4).
2973
According to the Panji system, how many paternal generations must 
be avoided for a marriage to be considered valid?
Seven generations.
2974
In the context of Maithil marriage eligibility, how many maternal 
generations must be avoided?
Five generations.
2975
Define the term 'Udegh' in the Panji system.
The detailed introduction of 32 ancestral roots (mools) used to 
verify marriage eligibility.
2976
Which historical ruler is credited with formalising the Panji 
Prabandh in 1326 AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva.
2977
Who was the first designated 'Panjikar' (registrar) appointed by 
Maharaja Harisimhadeva?
Pandit Gunakar Jha.
2978
The Panji system classifies Maithil Brahmins into three main 
hierarchical categories; name them.
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
2979
What is a 'Beeji Purush' in Maithil genealogy?
The earliest known common ancestor or 'root person' of a specific 
lineage or clan.
2980
How many 'Gotras' (clans) are traditionally identified within the 
Maithil Brahmin community?
20 Gotras.
2981
List the seven most populous or well-known Gotras among Maithil 
Brahmins.
Sandilya, Vatsa, Kashyapa, Savarna, Parashara, Bharadwaja, and 
Katyayana.
2982
In Maithil tradition, what are 'Pravars'?
The specific sage-ancestors associated with a Gotra who were 
composers of Vedic hymns.
2983
Explain the difference between 'Tri-Pravar' and 'Panch-Pravar'.
Tri-Pravar refers to lineages with three sage-ancestors, while Panch-
Pravar refers to those with five.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
2984
How many main 'Mools' (ancestral roots) were initially identified in 
the formal Panji system?
167 Mools.
2985
The Ayanth Shrestha category represents which level of Mool 
hierarchy?
The first or highest category of noble lineages.
2986
What triggered the formalisation of the Panji system according to 
legend involving Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya?
He inadvertently married a close relative (his cousin's daughter) due 
to a lack of genealogical records.
2987
Define 'Siddhanta' in the context of Maithil marriage.
A formal certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming that the bride 
and groom are not related within prohibited degrees.
2988
The certificate of non-relationship required for marriage is also 
known as _____.
Asvajana-patra
2989
What is the 'Chhadwith' (Sixteen Points) check used for?
To identify 16 specific female ancestral lines of the bride to ensure 
they do not overlap with the groom's paternal side.
2990
In the Panji system, what does 'Jaywar' refer to?
Brahmins who were not formally recorded in the initial Panji or 
who lost their status.
2991
Which Veda and Shakha (branch) are followed by 'Vajasaneyi' 
Maithil Brahmins?
The Madhyandina branch of the Shukla Yajurveda.
2992
Maithil Brahmins following the Samaveda belong to which specific 
branch?
The Kauthuma Shakha (Chhandogya).
2993
According to the source, why did Panji maintenance decline among 
Kshatriyas over time?
The practice was lost, leading them to simply avoid marrying into 
the same village to prevent prohibited unions.
2994
What is the significance of the year $1326$ AD ($1248$ Shaka) in 
this context?
It is the date the formal Panji system was established under state 
authority in Mithila.
2995
How does the Panji define a 'Sapinda' relationship?
Relationship through shared blood or ancestry within seven 
generations on the father's side and five on the mother's.
2996
What are the 'Pancha-Gauda' Brahmin divisions mentioned in the 
text?
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
2997
Which mountain range acts as the traditional geographic divider 
between Pancha-Gauda and Pancha-Dravida Brahmins?
The Vindhya Mountains.
2998
What is the 'Udegh' of the bride's 'Prapitamaha' (Great-grandfather) 
considered in the Chhadwith check?
The first Chhadwith (checkpoint).
2999
The 16th Chhadwith checkpoint involves which maternal ancestor 
of the bride?
The Matri-matamahi (mother's maternal grandmother's mother).
3000
Why is the preservation of 'Tirhuta' script considered essential to the 
Panji system?
Because almost all original Panji manuscripts are written in this 
specific script.
3001
Identify the primary source mentioned that provided the theoretical 
basis for 'Samuh-Lekhya' (community records) in the 7th century.
Kumarila Bhatta's 'Tantra-Varttik'.
3002
What does the Panji record indicate about social flexibility in 
medieval Mithila?
It contains numerous instances of inter-caste marriages and the 
acceptance of their descendants into the community over time.
3003
The 'Laukika' refers specifically to the Panji records of which 
group?
The Shrotriya Brahmins.
3004
How many 'Chaurala' (classes) are the Shrotriya Panjis divided into? Eight classes.
3005
According to the text, what is the 'Genome Mapping' aspect of the 
Panji records?
The systematic tracking of DNA/bloodlines through meticulous 
genealogical documentation over 1500 years.
3006
What advice is given to Panjikars to prevent the loss of the Panji 
tradition?
To train students and avoid excessive commercialism/greed in their 
duties.
3007
Which ancestor represents the 'Matamaha' in a lineage check?
The maternal grandfather.
3008
The Sandilya Gotra identifies with which specific Pravars?
Sandilya, Asit, and Deval.
3009
Which Gotra includes the Pravars: Shakti, Vashishtha, and 
Parashara?
Parashara Gotra.
3010
The text mentions a specific lineage checking period known as 
'Lion in Leo' (Singh-rashi Sun); what is its practical purpose?
It is the time recommended to dry and preserve the palm-leaf Panji 
manuscripts in the sun.
3011
What is the significance of the root 'Brahampura' in the text?
It is a rare example of a Mool (ancestral village) that is found across 
multiple different Gotras.
3012
Who was 'Udayanacharya' according to the records?
A famous scholar associated with the Pariharpur-Kariyan Mool of 
the Sandilya Gotra.
3013
Which scholar's lineage is traced back to MM Trianand Bhatt (c. 
450 AD)?
MM Halayudha.
3014
What was the 'Tantra-Varttik's' view on lineage preservation?
High-born families should maintain community records (Samuh-
Lekhya) to remember their ancestors' qualities and flaws.
3015
In the Panji terminology, what is a 'Kut-mamy'?
Children of one's maternal uncle, with whom marriage is strictly 
prohibited.
3016
What does the term 'Anshaj' refer to in the Panji categories?
Lineages that are recorded in the Panji but hold a rank below 
Shrotriya and Yogya.
3017
What is 'Patra Panji'?
Records used since roughly 500 years ago that focus on the 'Mool-
gram' (original village) details.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
3018
Which historical figure's mother is identified in the Panji as a 
'Devadasi' (temple dancer)?
The poet Vidyapati Thakur (as per specific historical/social 
notations in certain Panjis).
3019
According to the Panji, what is the consequence of marrying within 
a prohibited degree ('Svajana')?
The offspring were traditionally considered 'Chandala' or social 
outcasts from the Aryan fold.
3020
Maithil Brahmins primarily belong to which two Vedas?
Yajurveda and Samaveda.
3021
What distinguishes the Samavedic marriage ritual from the 
Yajurvedic one in Mithila?
Samavedic (Chhandogya) marriage has a specific 'Uttar-Vivah' 
segment involving the sighting of the Arundhati star.
3022
Which Gotra is associated with the Pravars: Kaushika, Atri, and 
Jamadagni?
Kaushika Gotra.
3023
Name the specific Mool and Gotra of the first Panjikar, Gunakar 
Jha.
Mohiji-Saurath (branch of Kharauri Mool), Sandilya Gotra.
3024
What is 'Sapinda-Nivritti'?
The point at which individuals are no longer considered 'Sapinda' 
(blood relatives) and can legally marry.
3025
Why did Panjis start being written on paper instead of palm leaves?
Due to the scarcity of palm leaves and the advent of modern writing 
materials, though old records remain on 'Tal-patra'.
3026
Term: Gotra-Pravar-Nirnaya
Definition: The formal determination of a person's clan and 
ancestral sages, required before any religious rite or marriage.
3027
Which historical region is the Panji system native to?
Mithila (North Bihar, India, and parts of Nepal).
3028
What was the primary purpose for the creation of the Panji 
Prabandha in the late medieval period?
To maintain the purity of lineage and caste for Brahmins, 
Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
3029
In the Mithila Panji system, what does 'Udegh' signify?
The detailed introduction of seven generations of ancestors to verify 
genealogical purity.
3030
How many specific 'kulas' (ancestral lineages) must be identified 
for a complete Udegh introduction?
32
3031
What is the minimum number of generations that must be avoided 
for marriage on the paternal side according to Panji rules?
Seven generations.
3032
In the context of Mithila marriage laws, how many generations 
must be avoided on the maternal side?
Five generations.
3033
Identify the three main social hierarchical categories of Maithil 
Brahmins created through the Panji system.
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibadh.
3034
What term is used for those individuals whose names are not 
recorded in the official Panji records?
Jayabar.
3035
How many total 'Gotras' (clans) are traditionally identified within 
the Maithil Brahmin community?
20
3036
How many 'Moola Grams' (original ancestral villages) were primary 
identified out of the 167 documented?
34 (later expanded to 35).
3037
The Maithil Brahmin community is vertically divided into two 
Vedic groups based on their rituals: the Chhandogya and the _____.
Vajasaneyi
3038
Which specific branch of the Samaveda is followed by the 
Chhandogya Brahmins of Mithila?
Kauthuma
3039
Which branch of the Shukla Yajurveda is followed by the 
Vajasaneyi Brahmins of Mithila?
Madhyandina
3040
Into how many total regional categories does the Purana divide the 
entire Brahmin community of India?
10
3041
What are the two broad categories used to group the ten regional 
Brahmin communities of India?
Panch Gauda and Panch Dravida.
3042
Which mountain range acts as the geographical boundary between 
the Panch Gauda and Panch Dravida Brahmins?
Vindhyachal
3043
Name the five Brahmin communities belonging to the 'Panch 
Gauda' group.
Utkala, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
3044
What was the ancient name of the kingdom that eventually became 
known as Mithila?
Videha
3045
In the Chhandogya marriage tradition, when is the 'Uttara Vivaha' 
performed?
After sunset, following the sighting of the star Arundhati.
3046
What is the defining characteristic of a 'Tri-Pravara' Gotra?
The lineage claims three ancestors who composed hymns in the 
Rigveda.
3047
What is the defining characteristic of a 'Panch-Pravara' Gotra?
The lineage claims five ancestors who composed hymns in the 
Rigveda.
3048
Which two Gotras in Mithila are forbidden from intermarrying 
because they share common ancestors despite being separate clans?
Savarna and Vatsa.
3049
What is the 'Samuha Lekhya', as mentioned by the philosopher 
Kumarila Bhatta?
A collective written record used by noble families to preserve their 
lineage and history.
3050
In which century was the Panji system officially formalised under 
royal patronage in Mithila?
The 14th Century (specifically 1326 AD).
3051
Which King of the Karnat dynasty is credited with the formal 
organisation of the Mithila Panji system?
King Harisimhadev.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
3052
Who was the scholar appointed by King Harisimhadev to manage 
the Panji records for Maithil Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
3053
What is the name of the certificate issued by a Panjikar confirming 
that a bride and groom are not related within prohibited degrees?
Siddhanta Patra (or Aswajanayapar).
3054
What is the specific role of a 'Panjikar' in Maithil society?
To act as a genealogist who verifies marriage eligibility and 
maintains family records.
3055
Concept: Biji Purusha
The earliest known progenitor or founder of a specific family 
lineage or Moola.
3056
Which script was traditionally used to write and maintain the Panji 
records?
Tirhuta (or Mithilakshar).
3057
What is a 'Dushana Panji'?
A specific record documenting faults, blemish, or prohibited 
incidents within a lineage.
3058
In the hierarchy of Maithil Brahmins, what is the 'Laukik' 
associated with?
The Shrotriya category's specific genealogical and social status 
records.
3059
How many main Shrotriya 'Panjis' are documented in the system?
32
3060
According to the text, why should Panji books be dried when the 
Sun enters the Leo (Simha) constellation?
Because the intense heat during this period helps preserve the palm 
leaves from moisture and decay.
3061
What are the three things a Panji book must be protected from, 
according to the traditional Sanskrit verse?
Water, oil, and loose binding.
3062
The term 'Moola' in the Panji system refers to the original _____ of 
a family.
Village or ancestral home.
3063
Which Maithil King initiated the 'Shakha Panji' to categorise 
families into sub-groups like Shrotriya?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
3064
In the Karna Kayastha community, which single Gotra is 
universally shared by all members?
Kashyapa
3065
How many categories of 'Chhatth' (prohibited relationship points) 
are checked for a bride's eligibility?
16
3066
A marriage is prohibited if any of the 16 prohibited ancestors of the 
bride appear in the groom's _____ lineage within 7 generations.
Paternal
3067
If a prohibited ancestor of the bride appears in the groom's maternal 
lineage, is the marriage allowed?
Yes, provided the exclusion limit of 5 generations is met.
3068
Which scholar is cited as having mistakenly married his cousin due 
to the lack of Panji records before the 14th century reform?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya.
3069
What historical text by Jyotirishwar Thakur mentions King 
Harisimhadev and the 72 Rajput clans?
Varna Ratnakar
3070
Which famous Maithili poet's genealogy is recorded in the Panji for 
over 19 generations?
Vidyapati Thakur.
3071
What is the title given to a Panjikar who passes the 'Maartand' or 
'Dhaut' examination?
Panji Shastra Maartand.
3072
The 'Tirhuta' script is described as the _____ of the Panji 
Prabandha system.
Life force (Pranadhara).
3073
What modern scientific concept does the author compare the Panji 
system to in the title of the work?
Genome Mapping
3074
How does the 'Shakha Panji' differ from the 'Moola Panji'?
Shakha Panji records branches and social status (like Shrotriya), 
whereas Moola Panji records basic ancestral origins.
3075
Why did Panjikars traditionally travel from village to village?
To collect and update information regarding new births and 
marriages.
3076
According to the rules of 'Sapinda', marriage is prohibited with the 
children of one's maternal _____.
Uncle (Mousi/Mama children).
3077
How many 'Panch Dravida' Brahmin groups are there according to 
the Purana?
Five (Karnataka, Gurjara, Tailanga, Dravida, and Maharashtra).
3078
What is the primary material traditionally used to write Panji 
records before the use of paper?
Palm leaves (Talpatra).
3079
What is the 'Asvajanayapar' certificate?
An official declaration that the two parties are not 'Swajana' 
(relatives) and are fit to marry.
3080
Which Gotra includes the Moola 'Khadaure', 'Khauade', and 
'Budhabade'?
Kashyapa (or similar high-ranking categories depending on the 
Moola).
3081
True or False: The Panji system originally applied to Kshatriyas as 
well as Brahmins.
True
3082
The Maithil Brahmin society is 'Pitri Pradhana', meaning lineages 
are tracked through the _____.
Father
3083
Who was the author of the 'Smritisara', a key text for Dharma 
Shastra mentioned in the source?
Pandit Harinath Upadhyaya.
3084
The Panji records were designed to prevent 'Chandala' offspring, 
which resulted from _____ marriages.
Prohibited or 'Swajana' (endogamous within restricted degrees).
3085
Which scholar's 19th-generation descendant is the Panjikar 
Vidyanand Jha?
Sadu-upadhyaya Gunakar Jha.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
3086
The process of verifying 32 lineages before marriage is called 
_____.
Udegh
3087
What was the purpose of the 'Dushana Panji' specifically regarding 
social standing?
To warn against lineages that had mixed with 'unknown' or 
'prohibited' groups.
3088
Why is the palm leaf manuscript of 'Devanand Chhoti Jha' 
significant?
It is considered one of the best-preserved and most accurate Shakha 
Panjis from the 18th century.
3089
What color of ink was traditionally used for the Tirhuta manuscripts 
of Panji?
Black (made from jungle herbs).
3090
What is the term for a family branch that has moved from its 
original 'Moola' village to a new village?
Dih
3091
In the Shrotriya hierarchy, which rank is considered the highest?
The First Category (Prathama Shreni).
3092
How many 'Pravaras' does the Shandilya Gotra have?
Three (Shandilya, Asit, and Deval).
3093
Which Gotra features five Pravaras: Aurva, Chyavana, Bhargava, 
Jamadagnya, and Apnuvana?
Vatsa (and also Savarna).
3094
What does 'Sapiá¹‡á¸ a' mean in the context of Hindu marriage laws? Sharing the same 'Pinda' or ancestral body/offering, signifying a 
blood relationship.
3095
The 20th Gotra listed in the Maithil Brahmin tradition is _____.
Jatukarna
3096
What is the role of 'Maha-Maho-Upadhyaya' in the Panji records?
It is a high academic and religious title used to denote distinguished 
scholars in the genealogy.
3097
According to the Panji system, can a groom marry his mother's 
brother's daughter?
No, it is strictly prohibited.
3098
In the Vajasaneyi branch, which four sacraments (Samskaras) are 
performed together?
Chudakarana, Upanayana, Vedarambha, and Samavartana.
3099
The Chhandogya branch excludes which sacrament that the 
Vajasaneyis include during the initiation phase?
Vedarambha
3100
Which dynasty's fall is associated with the rise of the Panji system 
as a protective social measure?
The Karnat Dynasty.
3101
Term: Bijipurbha
The progenitor of a specific village branch within a larger clan.
3102
What is the main consequence of not having a 'Siddhanta' before a 
Maithil wedding?
The marriage is considered 'Ashastriya' (unscriptural) and invalid 
by society.
3103
How many total 'Moolas' are identified across all Maithil Brahmins 
in the documented collection?
167
3104
Which historical ruler is credited with the systematic 
implementation of the Panji Prabandh?
Maharaja Harisingh Dev of the Karnat dynasty.
3105
In which year (AD) was the Panji system officially organised in 
Mithila?
In $1326$ AD (Shaka Era $1248$).
3106
What event prompted Maharaja Harisingh Dev to formalise the 
genealogical records?
The accidental marriage of the scholar Pandit Harinath Upadhyay to 
his first cousin due to a lack of records.
3107
The document issued by a Panjikar certifying that a couple is not 
related within prohibited degrees is called the _____.
Siddhant Patra (or Asvajana Patra).
3108
What is the difference between a 'Mool' and a 'Moolgram'?
Mool refers to the root clan, while Moolgram refers to the specific 
village where that branch settled.
3109
How many distinct 'Gotras' are recognised among Maithil Brahmins 
according to the source?
There are $20$ recognised Gotras.
3110
What does the term 'Udhed' refer to in the context of marriage 
verification?
The detailed introduction and lineage of $32$ specific ancestors 
(à¤•à¥à¤²) required to check for relationship overlaps.
3111
According to the Panji rules, how many generations must be 
avoided on the maternal side for a valid marriage?
Five generations.
3112
Which philosopher mentioned 'Samuh-lekhya' (collective records) 
in the 7th century to justify lineage protection?
Kumarila Bhatta in his work 'Tantravartika'.
3113
The highest hierarchical category of Maithil Brahmins established 
during the Panji Prabandh is known as _____.
Shrotriya.
3114
What are the three main hierarchical ranks of Maithil Brahmins 
mentioned in the text?
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddh.
3115
Why is marriage prohibited between individuals of the 'Vatsa' and 
'Savarna' Gotras?
Because they share the same primary ancestors, making them 
'Svajana' (relatives).
3116
What is the distinction between 'Tri-pravar' and 'Panch-pravar' 
families?
Tri-pravar families have three Rishi ancestors, while Panch-pravar 
have five.
3117
How many total 'Mools' (clans) were initially identified and 
recorded in the Panji system?
$167$ Mools.
3118
Which Veda and branch (Shakha) are followed by the 'Vajasaneyi' 
Brahmins of Mithila?
The Madhyandina branch of the Shukla Yajurveda.
3119
Which Veda and branch (Shakha) are followed by the 'Chhandogya' 
Brahmins of Mithila?
The Kauthuma branch of the Samaveda.
3120
How do the marriage rituals of Chhandogya and Vajasaneyi 
Brahmins differ regarding timing?
Chhandogya marriages are performed in two parts and require 
viewing the Arundhati star, whereas Vajasaneyi marriages have no 
such division.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
3121
What are the 'Panch-Gaud' Brahmin divisions mentioned in the 
Puranas?
Utkal, Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
3122
According to traditional preservation rules, what substances should 
a Panji book be protected from?
Water, oil, and loose or weak binding.
3123
When is the ideal time to sun-dry and air the genealogical Panji 
books?
When the Sun enters the constellation of Leo (typically mid-August 
to mid-September).
3124
Who was the first Panjikar appointed for the Maithil Brahmin 
community?
Pandit Gunakar Jha.
3125
Which Gotra is exclusively followed by the 'Karna Kayastha' 
community of Mithila?
Kashyap Gotra.
3126
How many Rajput clans are listed in the 'Varnaratnakara' context 
within the Panji system?
It mentions $72$ clans, with detailed descriptions for $64$ of them.
3127
The script traditionally used to write the Panji records is _____.
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
3128
What does the text refer to as 'Chhadwith'?
The sixteen specific ancestral points (such as maternal grandfather's 
maternal grandfather) used to calculate marriage eligibility.
3129
Under whose reign was the 'Shakha Panji' (branched records) 
introduced to create further sub-divisions?
Maharaja Madhav Singh.
3130
What is the penalty for a Brahmin whose lineage is not found in the 
official Panji records?
They are categorised as 'Jaywar' and placed outside the formal 
hierarchical ranks.
3131
The term for a marriage that occurs within prohibited genealogical 
degrees is _____.
Svajana (or related) marriage.
3132
Why does the text title the Panji system as 'Genome Mapping'?
To draw a parallel between ancient genealogical record-keeping and 
modern genetic science in preventing inbreeding.
3133
Which 'Mool' was added as the 35th main lineage in later periods?
The Khatam branch of the Phandah Mool.
3134
What is the significance of the number $32$ in the 'Udhed' process? It represents the total number of maternal and paternal ancestors 
checked for genealogical overlaps.
3135
Identify the two broad geographic categories of Indian Brahmins 
separated by the Vindhyachal mountains.
Panch-Gaud (North) and Panch-Dravid (South).
3136
How does the text define 'Sapanidya'?
The state of being related through a common ancestor, which 
prohibits marriage within specific generational limits.
3137
What are the four Upanayana (thread ceremony) rituals for 
Chhandogya Brahmins?
Namkaran, Chudakaran, Upanayana, and Samavartana.
3138
What are the four Upanayana rituals for Vajasaneyi Brahmins?
Chudakaran, Upanayana, Vedarambha, and Samavartana.
3139
The founding ancestor of the Panji-preservation lineage, who lived 
around $450$ AD, was _____.
Mahamahopadhyaya Trivikram Bhatta.
3140
What is a 'Laukik' in the context of Shrotriya Panjis?
A record of social status and family history specific to the Shrotriya 
category.
3141
How many total 'Laukik' or Shrotriya Panjis are recorded?
$32$ Panjis.
3142
How many 'Yogya' and 'Panjibaddh' Panjis are recorded in total?
$153$ Panjis.
3143
Who was the first Panjikar appointed for the 'Kshatriya' community? Vijayadatta.
3144
Who was the first Panjikar appointed for the 'Karna Kayastha' 
community?
Shankardatta.
3145
Term: Udhardvadhi
Definition: The vertical division of Brahmins based on the Veda 
and branch they follow.
3146
Which Maithil Brahmin Gotra lists 'Sandilya, Asit, and Deval' as its 
Pravar ancestors?
The Sandilya Gotra.
3147
Which Gotra includes 'Shakti, Vashistha, and Parashar' as its Pravar 
ancestors?
The Parashar Gotra.
3148
Which Gotra includes 'Kashyap, Avatsar, and Naidhruva' as its 
Pravar ancestors?
The Kashyap Gotra.
3149
What is the status of the 'Jaywar' category in the Panji system?
It refers to those who are recorded in the system but lack the 
specific merit or lineage status of higher ranks.
3150
What was the purpose of the 'Samuh-lekhya' as described by 
Kumarila Bhatta?
To prevent the loss of information regarding paternal and grand-
paternal traditions across generations.
3151
Concept: Adhogami Panji
Definition: The natural decline in family rank or status over time 
due to marriages into lower-ranked lineages.
3152
How did regional isolation (climate and language) affect the 
Brahmin community according to the text?
It led to the diversification of Brahmins into ten distinct regional 
groups (Gaud and Dravid).
3153
Which historical region is considered the administrative capital of 
the ancient Videha dynasty and the home of Maithil Brahmins?
Mithila.
3154
What was the primary language/script used for the 'Tantravartika' 
by Kumarila Bhatta?
Sanskrit.
3155
The term for family records that track only the primary root of the 
family is _____.
Mool Panji.
3156
The term for family records that track the various expanding 
branches of a lineage is _____.
Shakha Panji.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
3157
Which king's reign saw the introduction of 'Srotriya' and 'Yogya' 
divisions as social classifications?
The system was formalised under Harisingh Dev, but further 
stratified under later Darbhanga Raj rulers like Madhav Singh.
3158
The genealogical 'stain' resulting from a documented social or ritual 
error in a family is listed in which Panji?
The Dushana Panji.
3159
What role does the 'Biji Purush' play in modern genomic 
comparisons in the text?
He is viewed as the source of the specific Y-chromosome or genetic 
marker for the entire clan.
3160
How does the Panji system prevent 'inbreeding'?
By mathematically excluding $32$ ancestral lines to ensure genetic 
diversity in marital unions.
3161
The scholar who received the title 'Panji Shastra Martand' from 
Maharaja Kameshwar Singh was _____.
Modanand Jha.
3162
What was the primary purpose of the 'Panji Prabandha' system 
established in Mithila?
To ensure lineage purity among Brahmins, Kayasthas, and 
Kshatriyas by maintaining detailed genealogical records.
3163
During which historical period was the 'Panji Prabandha' system 
formally organised?
The late Medieval period, specifically in $1326$ AD (Saka $1248$).
3164
Which ruler is credited with the formal initiation and patronage of 
the Panji system?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva of the Karnat dynasty.
3165
What is the meaning of the term 'Udegh' in the context of Maithil 
genealogy?
The introduction of $32$ ancestral lineages (maternal and paternal) 
to verify marriage eligibility.
3166
In the Panji system, how many generations must be avoided for 
marriage on the maternal side?
Five generations.
3167
Name the three primary social categories that emerged from the 
Panji classification.
Shrotriya, Yogya, and Panjibaddha.
3168
What term is used for those who were not included in the formal 
Panji records?
Jaywar.
3169
How many 'Gotras' (clans) are officially recognized among Maithil 
Brahmins in the Panji system?
Twenty ($20$).
3170
How many 'Muls' (ancestral roots/villages) were initially 
documented in the Panji records?
One hundred and sixty-seven ($167$).
3171
In Maithil genealogy, what is a 'Beeji Purush'?
The founding or earliest known ancestor of a specific lineage.
3172
What does the term 'Mulgram' signify in the Panji system?
The original ancestral village of a family branch.
3173
How is the 'Pravara' of a Maithil Brahmin lineage determined?
By the number of sages (Rishis) in the lineage who are credited 
with composing Rigvedic hymns.
3174
Distinguish between 'Tri-pravara' and 'Panch-pravara'.
Tri-pravara lineages trace back to three sage ancestors, while Panch-
pravara trace to five.
3175
The Gotras Vatsa, Savarna, and Bharadwaj are categorized under 
which Pravara type?
Panch-pravara.
3176
The Gotras Shandilya, Kashyap, and Parashar are categorized under 
which Pravara type?
Tri-pravara.
3177
Why is marriage traditionally prohibited between individuals of the 
Vatsa and Savarna Gotras?
They share the same set of five ancestral sages (Aurva, Chyavana, 
Bhargava, Jamadagnya, and Apnuvana).
3178
Who was the first Panjikar (genealogist) appointed for Maithil 
Brahmins?
Gunakar Jha.
3179
Who was the first official Panjikar for the Maithil Kshatriyas?
Vijaydatta.
3180
What is the 'Chhadisth' in the context of marriage verification?
A set of $16$ generational intersections checked by the Panjikar to 
ensure the couple is not related.
3181
What name is given to Maithil Brahmins who follow the 
Madhyandina branch of the Yajurveda?
Vajasaneyi.
3182
Which marriage ritual is unique to the Chhandog Brahmins 
regarding the timing of the ceremony?
The second part of the marriage occurs after sunset upon sighting 
the star Arundhati.
3183
What is the 'Pancha Gauda' classification of Brahmins?
A group of five northern Brahmin communities: Utkal, 
Kanyakubja, Gauda, Maithil, and Saraswat.
3184
How does the Panji define the 'Pancha Dravida' Brahmins?
Brahmins residing south of the Vindhyachal mountains: Karnataka, 
Gurjar, Tailang, Dravid, and Maharashtra.
3185
What was the 'Samuh Lekhya' mentioned by the philosopher 
Kumarila Bhatta?
An early form of written collective genealogy used by high-born 
families to protect their caste identity.
3186
According to the text, what is the consequence of marrying a 
'Svajana' (forbidden relative)?
The offspring are traditionally considered 'Chandals' and the family 
is excluded from the Aryan social order.
3187
The 'Dushana Panji' is specifically used to record what kind of 
information?
Social or lineage 'defects' or 'stains' that occurred in a family's 
history.
3188
How many main 'Muls' were eventually distilled into the primary 
category of high-lineage Brahmins?
Thirty-four ($34$), which later became $35$ with the inclusion of 
the Khanam branch.
3189
What is the historical significance of the philosopher Kumarila 
Bhatta in the context of Panji history?
He mentioned 'Samuh Lekhya' in his work 'Tantra Varttika' as a 
means of preserving lineage purity.
3190
In the Chhadisth calculation, the first Chhadisth refers to which 
relative of the bride?
The father of the bride's great-great-grandfather ($Vriddha \ 
Pranishamah$).
3191
Why is the year $1326$ AD crucial for the Panji system?
It marks the formal registration and centralisation of Maithil 
genealogy under royal decree.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
3192
Which traditional script is used to maintain the original Panji 
records?
Tirhuta (Mithilakshar).
3193
The 'Gotra Panji' contains information regarding which two 
elements?
The Gotras and their associated ancient Muls (roots).
3194
The rule 'Matritah Panchami Tyaktwa Pitritah Saptami Bhajet' 
refers to what?
The prohibition of marriage within five generations of the mother 
and seven of the father.
3195
Under whose orders was the 'Shakha Panji' (branch genealogy) 
formally commissioned in the $18^{th}$ century?
Maharaja Madhav Singh of Darbhanga.
3196
What is the 'Brahmapura' Mul uniquely known for in the Panji 
system?
It is an exception that is found in multiple Gotras, such as 
Shandilya, Vatsa, and Kashyap.
3197
The term 'Vajasaneyi' refers to those following the branch of which 
Veda?
Shukla Yajurveda.
3198
Which historical figure, known as the 'Maithil Poet Laureate', has 
his $7$ generations of ancestors detailed in the Panji?
Vidyapati Thakur.
3199
How are Shrotriya Brahmins categorized within their Panji?
They are divided into eight 'Chauwala' (categories) and $32$ 
specific Panjis.
3200
In the Panji terminology, what does 'Adhogami' signify regarding 
lineage status?
The downward movement of a family's social rank due to improper 
marriage alliances.
3201
What is the function of the 'Udegh Panji'?
It provides knowledge of the 'Sapinda' relationships (lineage 
proximity) up to six generations.
3202
Identify the primary sages associated with the Shandilya Gotra.
Shandilya, Asit, and Deval.
3203
What are the three components of a 'Gotra' definition as per the 
Amarkosh?
Mountain, Lineage ($Vansha$), and Name.
3204
How did the Panji system adapt to the decline of memory-based 
lineage tracking?
By institutionalising the role of the Panjikar and creating written 
state-verified records.
3205
What is the 'Pancha Aditya Kula' mentioned in the Panji dedication? A high-born lineage grouping that the Panji records aim to keep 
'pure' through verification.
3206
According to the Panji theory, what is the 'seed' of Dharma?
The 'Varna' (community), which in turn is rooted in the purity of 
birth ($Janma$).
3207
Which text is cited to prove that high-born individuals go to great 
lengths to protect their lineage?
The 'Tantra Varttika' by Kumarila Bhatta.
3208
What role does the 'Pravara' list play during the thread ceremony 
(Upayana)?
It is used to invoke the ancestral sages during the ritual offerings.
3209
Name the four specific Samskaras (rituals) distinguished between 
Chhandog and Vajasaneyi traditions.
Namakarana, Chudakarana, Upanayana, and Samavartana (with 
variations in the fourth step).
3210
How many total Panjis exist for the Yogya and Panjibaddha 
categories?
One hundred and fifty-three ($153$).
3211
Which city served as the original capital of the Videha dynasty, the 
cultural precursor to Mithila?
Mithila (the region eventually became synonymous with the 
capital's name).
3212
The 'Panji-Prabandha' was created during the reign of which 
dynasty?
The Karnat dynasty.
3213
What was the significance of the year Saka $1019$ mentioned in 
the text?
It marks a historical point regarding the establishment of the Karnat 
dynasty's rule over Mithila.
3214
What is the meaning of 'Sapinda' in the context of Hindu marriage 
laws practiced in Mithila?
Relatives who share the same 'Pinda' (ancestral offering), implying 
common blood lineage.
3215
In the Panji system, what happens if an individual's name is not 
found in the genealogical books?
They lose their status as 'Panjibaddha' and may be treated as 
'Jaywar'.
3216
Who is the primary scholar responsible for the digital imaging and 
editing of the modern Panji Theory compilation?
Gajendra Thakur and Nagendra Kumar Jha.
3217
Why is the preservation of Panji books considered a sacred duty in 
the text?
They are the 'lifeblood' of the Tirhuta script and the only surviving 
$1000$-year-old genealogical network in the world.
3218
What advice is given for the physical preservation of Panji 
manuscripts?
Protect them from water, oil, and loose binding, and dry them 
during the Sun's transit in Leo (August/September).
3219
What is the 'Ayan' (category) considered most superior in the 
second à¤¶à¥à¤°à¥‡à¤£à¥€ (rank) of Muls?
Lineages like Ranwallan, Paliwallan, and Kujawallan.
3220
How does the Panji account for the geographical spread of 
Brahmins into 'Pancha Gauda'?
Differences in climate, language, food, and dress in various regions 
led to the distinct cultural identities of these five groups.
3221
What is the unique feature of the 'Shakha Panji' of Darbhanga 
Maharaja Madhav Singh?
It created a permanent separation between the Shrotriya, Yogya, 
and Panjibaddha ranks.
3222
In the context of the Panji system, what is the primary purpose of 
'Panji Prabandh'?
It is a genealogical record maintained to ensure the lineage purity 
and social standing of Brahmins, Kayasthas, and Kshatriyas.
3223
Which ruler is credited with the formal standardisation and 
institutionalisation of the Panji records in 1326 AD?
Maharaja Harisimhadeva of the Karnat dynasty.
3224
Define the term 'Uderh' within Maithili genealogical traditions.
It refers to the detailed introduction of an individual's ancestry, 
covering seven generations and thirty-two distinct family lines.
3225
What event reportedly triggered the necessity for recorded Panji 
under Maharaja Harisimhadeva?
The scholar Pandit Hari Nath Upadhyay inadvertently married his 
own maternal cousin due to a lack of genealogical knowledge.
3226
How many 'Gotras' (clans) are traditionally identified among 
Maithili Brahmins according to the text?
There are twenty distinct Gotras.
